examples.title: Algorithm: Deque Implementation using Circular Array
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating how to implement a deque with insert and delete operations at both ends using a circular array approach
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates a circular array implementation of a deque. Key concepts: (1) front and rear pointers track both ends, (2) circular arithmetic using modulo (%) allows wrap-around, (3) insertFront moves front backward, (4) insertRear moves rear forward, (5) deleteFront moves front forward, (6) deleteRear moves rear backward, (7) isFull condition is when next position of front equals rear.

examples.title: Python: Deque Implementation with All Operations
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of a deque using a circular array with insertFront, insertRear, deleteFront, deleteRear, and utility methods
examples.code:
"""
Double-Ended Queue (Deque) Implementation using Circular Array
VSU 2022 Scheme - CSE Topic: Double-Ended Queues
"""

class Deque:
 def __init__(self, max_size):
 """Initialize deque with given maximum size"""
 self.max_size = max_size
 self.array = [None] * max_size
 self.front = -1 # Points to front element
 self.rear = -1 # Points to rear element
 
 def is_empty(self):
 """Check if deque is empty"""
 return self.front == -1
 
 def is_full(self):
 """Check if deque is full using circular condition"""
 return (self.rear + 1) % self.max_size == self.front
 
 def insert_front(self, element):
 """Insert element at the front of deque"""
 if self.is_full():
 print("Deque Overflow! Cannot insert at front.")
 return False
 
 # If deque is empty
 if self.front == -1:
 self.front = 0
 self.rear = 0
 else:
 # Move front backward (circular)
 self.front = (self.front - 1 + self.max_size) % self.max_size
 
 self.array[self.front] = element
 return True
 
 def insert_rear(self, element):
 """Insert element at the rear of deque"""
 if self.is_full():
 print("Deque Overflow! Cannot insert at rear.")
 return False
 
 # If deque is empty
 if self.front == -1:
 self.front = 0
 self.rear = 0
 else:
 # Move rear forward (circular)
 self.rear = (self.rear + 1) % self.max_size
 
 self.array[self.rear] = element
 return True
 
 def delete_front(self):
 """Remove and return element from front"""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Deque Underflow! Cannot delete from front.")
 return None
 
 element = self.array[self.front]
 self.array[self.front] = None # Help garbage collection
 
 # If only one element existed
 if self.front == self.rear:
 self.front = -1
 self.rear = -1
 else:
 # Move front forward (circular)
 self.front = (self.front + 1) % self.max_size
 
 return element
 
 def delete_rear(self):
 """Remove and return element from rear"""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Deque Underflow! Cannot delete from rear.")
 return None
 
 element = self.array[self.rear]
 self.array[self.rear] = None # Help garbage collection
 
 # If only one element existed
 if self.front == self.rear:
 self.front = -1
 self.rear = -1
 else:
 # Move rear backward (circular)
 self.rear = (self.rear - 1 + self.max_size) % self.max_size
 
 return element
 
 def get_front(self):
 """Return front element without removing"""
 if self.is_empty():
 return None
 return self.array[self.front]
 
 def get_rear(self):
 """Return rear element without removing"""
 if self.is_empty():
 return None
 return self.array[self.rear]
 
 def size(self):
 """Return current number of elements"""
 if self.front == -1:
 return 0
 elif self.rear >= self.front:
 return self.rear - self.front + 1
 else:
 return self.max_size - self.front + self.rear + 1
 
 def display(self):
 """Display current deque contents"""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Deque is empty")
 return
 
 result = []
 if self.rear >= self.front:
 for i in range(self.front, self.rear + 1):
 result.append(str(self.array[i]))
 else:
 for i in range(self.front, self.max_size):
 result.append(str(self.array[i]))
 for i in range(0, self.rear + 1):
 result.append(str(self.array[i]))
 
 print("Deque contents:", " <-> ".join(result))
 print(f"Front={self.front}, Rear={self.rear}, Size={self.size()}")


# Demo: Testing Deque Operations
if __name__ == "__main__":
 dq = Deque(5)
 
 print("=== Deque Operations Demo ===\n")
 
 # Insert operations
 dq.insert_rear(10)
 dq.insert_front(20)
 dq.insert_rear(30)
 dq.insert_front(40)
 dq.display()
 
 print("\n--- Deletion Operations ---")
 print(f"Deleted from front: {dq.delete_front()}")
 dq.display()
 print(f"Deleted from rear: {dq.delete_rear()}")
 dq.display()
 
 print("\n--- Peek Operations ---")
 print(f"Front element: {dq.get_front()}")
 print(f"Rear element: {dq.get_rear()}")
 
 print("\n--- Palindrome Check Demo ---")
 # Palindrome checker using deque
 def is_palindrome(string):
 d = Deque(len(string))
 for char in string.lower():
 d.insert_rear(char)
 
 while d.size() > 1:
 if d.delete_front() != d.delete_rear():
 return False
 return True
 
 print(f"'racecar' is palindrome: {is_palindrome('racecar')}")
 print(f"'hello' is palindrome: {is_palindrome('hello')}")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation provides a complete deque class with all core operations. Key features: (1) Circular array ensures O(1) insertion/deletion at both ends, (2) isFull uses (rear+1)%max_size == front condition, (3) insertFront decrements front circularly, (4) insertRear increments rear circularly, (5) deleteFront and deleteRear handle single-element case, (6) Includes palindrome checker demonstration showing practical application.

examples.title: C: Deque Implementation with Circular Array
examples.description: Complete C implementation of a deque using circular array with proper memory management and all deque operations
examples.explanation: This C implementation provides a complete deque with circular array management. Key aspects: (1) Uses MAX_SIZE constant for array bounds, (2) front=-1 and rear=-1 indicate empty deque, (3) isFull uses modulo arithmetic: (rear+1)%MAX_SIZE == front, (4) insertFront decrements front with wrap-around using (front-1+MAX_SIZE)%MAX_SIZE, (5) insertRear increments rear with wrap-around, (6) deleteFront and deleteRear both handle single-element case, (7) Display traverses circularly from front to rear.
